helpful: fedcourts query --court scotus gave the realized disposition base rates (other 75% / dismissed 19% / denied 4.5% / granted 1.6%) that anchored the call
filtering query summaries for railroad/commission/mootness priors surfaced comparable historical dispositions
gaps: the snapshot carries an opinion-cluster URL but no way to read the cluster's disposition as legal context without it counting as a new case fact; a snapshot field that already classifies the linked cluster's disposition would disambiguate other-vs-dismissed
query date_decided/date_filed are null for these historical rows, so I could not weight priors by era
notes: Thin snapshot (empty docket_entries, all dates null) for a pre-1925 mandatory-jurisdiction SCOTUS matter. The published opinion cluster signals a merits/dismissal disposition rather than a discretionary denial; base rates plus the railroad-regulation posture drove a dismissed call with granted=0.
